dashboard_recap.headline: MLB Daily Recap - April 21, 2026
dashboard_recap.article:
**Astros Fall to Guardians, Red Sox Shut Down by Yankees**

The Houston Astros dropped a 5-8 decision to the Cleveland Guardians, led by Angel Martínez's 2-hit, 1-home run performance. Martínez's big hit came on a double to left field, scoring Brice Matthews. Carlos Correa and Yordan Alvarez also had strong games for the Astros.

In a dominant pitching performance, the New York Yankees blanked the Boston Red Sox 4-0. Giancarlo Stanton led the Yankees' offense with a 2-hit, 1-home run game, driving in three runs. Luis Gil earned the win for the Yankees, pitching 6.0 innings of shutout baseball.

The Cincinnati Reds also had a strong day, pounding out 12 runs in a 12-6 win over the Tampa Bay Rays. Elly De La Cruz led the Reds' offense with a 3-hit, 2-home run performance, driving in five runs. Chase Burns earned the win for the Reds, pitching 5.2 innings and striking out eight.

The Atlanta Braves were blown out by the Washington Nationals, 4-11. Curtis Mead had a big game for the Nationals, hitting a home run and driving in three runs. Mauricio Dubón also had a strong game for the Nationals, going 2-for-4.

The Minnesota Twins edged the New York Mets 5-3, led by Francisco Lindor's 1-hit, 1-home run performance. Nolan McLean earned the win for the Twins, pitching 6.2 innings and striking out ten.

The Chicago White Sox routed the Arizona Diamondbacks 11-5, led by Colson Montgomery's 2-hit, 1-home run performance. Jonathan Loáisiga earned the win for the White Sox, pitching 1.1 innings in relief.

Finally, the San Diego Padres edged the Colorado Rockies 1-0, thanks to a strong pitching performance from Chase Dollander. Dollander pitched 6.0 innings and struck out nine before the Padres' bullpen shut the door.
